# dynamodb-toolkit — Full Reference

> No-dependency CommonJS micro-library for AWS DynamoDB. Targets either `AWS.DynamoDB` or `AWS.DynamoDB.DocumentClient` (auto-detected via `typeof client.createSet == 'function'`). Provides the `Adapter` class, the `KoaAdapter` HTTP wrapper, and a set of standalone utilities for batch/transaction/pagination/patching/projection.

This file is the long-form companion to `llms.txt`. Read `llms.txt` first for the API summary; this file documents semantics, edge cases, and the full standalone utility API.

## 1. Adapter

`Adapter` (`Adapter.js`) is the central class. One Adapter typically maps to one logical entity (often one table). All write paths funnel through `make*()` builders and all reads honor `indirectIndices`.

### 1.1 Constructor

```js
const Adapter = require('dynamodb-toolkit');

const adapter = new Adapter({
  client,                          // required: AWS.DynamoDB or AWS.DynamoDB.DocumentClient
  table: 'planets',                // required: base table name
  keyFields: ['name'],             // required: partition key first, then sort key
  specialTypes: {tags: 1},         // optional: arrays stored as DynamoDB Sets, not Lists
  projectionFieldMap: {},          // optional: alias → real field for projections
  searchable: {name: 1},           // optional: fields with '-search-<name>' lowercase mirrors
  searchablePrefix: '-search-',    // optional: default '-search-'
  indirectIndices: {},             // optional: {IndexName: 1} for key-only GSIs
  converter: customConverter,      // optional: override utils/converter
  converterOptions: {convertEmptyValues: true},  // optional
  prepare(item, isPatch) { ... },
  revive(rawItem, fields) { ... },
  validateItem(item, isPatch) { ... },
  checkConsistency(batch) { ... }
});

// or the static helper:
const adapter = Adapter.make(options);
// Adapter.adapt is an alias of Adapter.make.
```

The constructor stores `this.isDocClient = typeof this.client.createSet == 'function'`. Most internal branches consult this flag to choose between `put`/`putItem`, etc.

### 1.2 Marker classes

- `Adapter.Raw` — caller-shaped JSON, possibly with technical fields. Skips `prepare()` but is still passed through `convertTo()` when the client is the raw `AWS.DynamoDB` (not the DocumentClient).
- `Adapter.DbRaw` — already in DynamoDB wire format (`{S: ...}` etc). Skips both `prepare()` and conversion.

Both have a static `make(source)` factory.

`adapter.markAsRaw(rawItem)` returns the right marker for the current client mode (`Raw` for DocumentClient, `DbRaw` for raw DynamoDB).

### 1.3 User-defined hooks (defaults shown)

```js
prepare(item, isPatch)        // returns item (identity)
prepareKey(key, index)        // returns this.restrictKey(this.prepare(key), index)
restrictKey(rawKey, index)    // returns subset of rawKey with this.keyFields
prepareListParams(item, index) // returns {}
updateParams(params, options) // returns params (identity); options: {name, force}
revive(rawItem, fields)       // subsetObject(rawItem, fields) if fields else rawItem
async validateItem(item, isPatch)  // no-op; throw to abort
async checkConsistency(batch)      // returns null; return more batch items to add a transaction
```

`updateParams` is called from `makePost` (`{name: 'post'}`), `makePut` (`{name: 'put', force}`), `makePatch` (`{name: 'patch'}`), and `makeDelete` (`{name: 'delete'}`).

### 1.4 Batch builders

Each returns a batch descriptor consumable by `applyTransaction` / `applyBatch`.

```js
await adapter.makeGet(key, fields, params)
// returns {action: 'get', adapter, params}

await adapter.makeCheck(key, params)
// returns {action: 'check', params}

await adapter.makePost(item)
// runs validateItem (unless item is Raw); adds existence check; returns {action: 'put', params}

await adapter.makePut(item, force, params)
// runs validateItem (unless Raw); existence check unless force; returns {action: 'put', params}

await adapter.makePatch(key, item, params)
// extracts item.__delete and item.__separator; runs validateItem (unless Raw/DbRaw);
// removes key fields from item; calls prepareUpdate; returns {action: 'patch', params}

await adapter.makeDelete(key, params)
// returns {action: 'delete', params}
```

`makePatch` honors marker classes for the `item`:
- `DbRaw` + DocumentClient → unmarshalls via `convertFrom`.
- `DbRaw` + raw client → passes through.
- `Raw` + DocumentClient → passes through.
- `Raw` + raw client → marshals via `convertTo`.
- Plain object → `validateItem`, then `prepare(item, true)`, then conversion if needed.

### 1.5 CRUD methods

```js
await adapter.get(item, fields, params, returnRaw, ignoreIndirection)
// alias: getByKey(key, fields, params, returnRaw, ignoreIndirection)
// returns the item, or undefined if not found

await adapter.post(item)
// inserts; throws if the partition key already exists

await adapter.put(item, force, params)
// upserts; without force, throws unless the item exists (by partition key)

await adapter.patch(item, params)
// alias: patchByKey(key, item, params); modifies the item identified by its key fields

await adapter.delete(key, params)
// alias: deleteByKey(key, params)

await adapter.clone(item, mapFn, force, params, returnRaw)
// alias: cloneByKey(key, mapFn, force, params, returnRaw)
// reads → mapFn(item) → post (or put when force=true). Returns true on success, false if not found.

await adapter.move(item, mapFn, force, params, returnRaw)
// alias: moveByKey(key, mapFn, force, params, returnRaw)
// like clone, but deletes the source in the same transaction as the write.
```

For each write, `checkConsistency(batch)` is consulted. If it returns truthy, `applyTransaction(client, checks, batch)` runs the whole thing as `transactWriteItems`. Otherwise the single op runs directly.

### 1.6 `returnRaw` semantics

- omitted / falsy → run `revive(rawItem, fields)`.
- `'raw'` → return `new Adapter.Raw(...)` after un-marshalling if needed.
- `'db-raw'` → return `new Adapter.DbRaw(...)` in wire format.

Use `'raw'` / `'db-raw'` when piping reads back into writes to avoid re-running `prepare`/`revive`.

### 1.7 Indirect indices

`indirectIndices: {IndexName: 1}` declares a GSI whose projected items are key-only. Affected reads:

1. First call queries the index with only `keyFields` projected.
2. Returned keys are reshaped via `restrictKey()` and run through `readOrderedListByKeys` against the base table to fetch full attributes.
3. `ignoreIndirection: true` bypasses the second hop.

This logic lives in `getByKey`, `getByKeys`, `scanAllByParams`, and `getAllByParams`.

### 1.8 Mass operations (native)

Use DynamoDB batch/transaction APIs for throughput.

```js
await adapter.scanAllByParams(params, fields, returnRaw, ignoreIndirection)
// returns {nextParams, items}; pass nextParams back in to continue.

await adapter.getAllByParams(params, options, returnRaw, ignoreIndirection)
// paginated read with offset/limit; returns {data, ...} from paginateList.

await adapter.getByKeys(keys, fields, params, returnRaw, ignoreIndirection)
// batchGet over keys; returns array.

await adapter.getAll(options, item, index, returnRaw, ignoreIndirection)
// builds params via makeListParams, then getAllByParams.

await adapter.putAll(items)
// writeList: chunked batchWrite with backoff.

await adapter.deleteAllByParams(params)
await adapter.deleteByKeys(keys)
await adapter.deleteAll(options, item, index)

await adapter.cloneAllByParams(params, mapFn, returnRaw)
await adapter.cloneByKeys(keys, mapFn, returnRaw)
await adapter.cloneAll(options, mapFn, item, index, returnRaw)

await adapter.moveAllByParams(params, mapFn, returnRaw)
await adapter.moveByKeys(keys, mapFn, returnRaw)
await adapter.moveAll(options, mapFn, item, index, returnRaw)
```

### 1.9 Mass operations (generic)

Sequential, item-by-item versions. Slower but lower memory and trivially correct under unusual schemas:

`genericGetByKeys`, `genericPutAll`, `genericDeleteAllByParams`, `genericDeleteByKeys`, `genericCloneAllByParams`, `genericCloneByKeys`, `genericMoveAllByParams`, `genericMoveByKeys`. All return the count of processed items where applicable.

### 1.10 Utility methods

```js
adapter.cloneParams(params)
// shallow clone of params with TableName forced to this.table.

adapter.cleanGetParams(params)
// cloneParams + delete ConditionExpression + cleanParams.

adapter.checkExistence(params, invert)
// adds attribute_exists(<partition key>) or attribute_not_exists; ANDs with any existing ConditionExpression.

adapter.makeParams(options, project, params, skipSelect)
// applies options: consistent → ConsistentRead, descending → ScanIndexForward=false,
// fields (string|array) → ProjectionExpression, filter → FilterExpression via filtering().

adapter.makeListParams(options, project, item, index)
// → makeParams(options, project, prepareListParams(item, index))

adapter.addKeyFields(params, skipSelect)
// adds keyFields to ProjectionExpression.

adapter.convertFrom(item, ignoreSpecialTypes)
adapter.convertTo(item, ignoreSpecialTypes)
// wire-format conversion; honors specialTypes unless ignoreSpecialTypes.

adapter.fromDynamo(item, fields, returnRaw)
adapter.toDynamo(item)
adapter.toDynamoKey(key, index)
adapter.fromDynamoRaw(item)
adapter.toDynamoRaw(item)
adapter.markAsRaw(rawItem)

await adapter.validateItems(items, isPatch)
```

## 2. KoaAdapter

`KoaAdapter` (`helpers/KoaAdapter.js`) is a thin HTTP wrapper:

```js
const KoaAdapter = require('dynamodb-toolkit/helpers/KoaAdapter');

const koa = new KoaAdapter(adapter, {
  sortableIndices: {name: '-t-name-index'},
  augmentItemFromContext(item, ctx) { ... },
  augmentCloneFromContext(ctx) { return item => ({...item, ...}); },
  async getAll(ctx) { ... }   // override per-route handlers
});
```

If the first arg is not already an `Adapter`, it's treated as Adapter constructor options and `new Adapter(...)` is called.

### 2.1 Hooks

- `augmentItemFromContext(item, ctx)` — defaults to `Object.assign(item, ctx.params)`.
- `augmentCloneFromContext(ctx)` — defaults to `item => ({...item, ...ctx.request.body})`.
- `extractKeys(ctx, forceQuery)` — returns an array of keys. Reads `ctx.request.body` for `PUT`/`POST` (must be an array of names, else throws). Otherwise reads `ctx.query.names` (comma-separated, else throws).
- `namesToKeys(ctx, names)` — defaults to `name => ({name})`. Override for compound keys.
- `makeOptions(ctx)` — `{consistent, filter, fields, offset, limit}` from `ctx.query`.
- `makeParams(ctx, project, params)` → `adapter.makeParams(makeOptions(ctx), project, params)`.

### 2.2 Handlers

Each is `async (ctx) => Promise<void>`. Successful writes set `ctx.status = 204`; missing items on read/clone/move set `ctx.status = 404`.

Item: `get`, `post`, `put`, `patch`, `delete`, `clone`, `move`, `doClone(ctx, mapFn)`, `doMove(ctx, mapFn)`.

Mass: `getAll`, `getByNames`, `deleteAll`, `deleteByNames`, `cloneAll`, `cloneByNames`, `moveAll`, `moveByNames`, plus `doCloneAll`, `doCloneByNames`, `doMoveAll`, `doMoveByNames`.

`force` (for `put`/`clone`/`move`) and `consistent` (for `get`/`getByNames`) are read from the query string via `helpers/isTrue.js` (`yes|true|1|on`).

### 2.3 Routing example

See `tests/routes.js` for the canonical Koa wiring against the Star Wars planets dataset.

## 3. Patching

`prepareUpdate(patch, deleteProps, params, separator = '.')`:

- Builds `UpdateExpression` `SET` clauses from `patch`'s own keys.
- Splits each key on `separator` for nested paths. Pure-digit segments are passed through as array indices and **not** aliased.
- Builds `REMOVE` clauses from `deleteProps` (an array of paths).
- Reuses any existing `ExpressionAttributeNames` / `ExpressionAttributeValues`, deduping aliases as `#upk<n>` / `:upv<n>`.
- Returns the mutated `params`.

`makePatch()` extracts `item.__delete` and `item.__separator` before calling `prepareUpdate`. Key fields are removed from the patch automatically.

```js
await adapter.patch({
  name: 'Alderaan',           // key field — stripped automatically
  diameter: 12500,
  'meta.tag': 'destroyed',    // dotted path → meta.tag
  __delete: ['climate'],      // REMOVE climate
  __separator: '.'            // optional, default '.'
});
```

## 4. Standalone utilities

Each file in `utils/` exports one main symbol. Import as `require('dynamodb-toolkit/utils/<name>')`.

### Client

- `makeClient(AWS, pragmas, options)` — returns `new AWS.DynamoDB.DocumentClient` if `pragmas.docClient`, else `new AWS.DynamoDB`. Pulls region/credentials from the AWS shared config/credentials files via `getProfileName()` unless `pragmas.profile` overrides. `pragmas.warn` enables console warnings.
  - `makeClient.getOptions(AWS, pragmas)` — just resolves the options without constructing.
- `createClient(AWS, profile, warn)` — thin wrapper: `makeClient(AWS, {profile, warn})`.
- `getProfileName()` — returns `process.env.AWS_PROFILE` or `process.env.AWS_DEFAULT_PROFILE`.

### Conversion

- `converter` — `{input, output, marshall, unmarshall}` mirroring `AWS.DynamoDB.Converter`. Supports `convertEmptyValues` and `useArraysForSets`.
- `convertTypes` — `{convertTo, convertFrom}` honoring a `specialTypes` map (which arrays are Sets).

### Batches and transactions

- `applyBatch(client, ...requests)` — chunked `batchWriteItem`; LIMIT 25 per call. Accepts batch descriptors `{action: 'put'|'delete', params}` (singletons or arrays). Returns the count.
- `applyTransaction(client, ...requests)` — `transactWriteItems` builder. Accepts `'put'|'delete'|'patch'|'check'` actions. Falls through silently when no items remain.
- `batchWrite(client, params)` / `batchGet(client, params)` — single-call wrappers with `backoff()` retries on unprocessed items.
- `getBatch(client, params)` — low-level batch reader used by `readListByKeys`.
- `getTransaction(client, ...requests)` — `transactGetItems` builder.
- `backoff(fn, attempt)` / `backoff.delay(attempt)` — exponential backoff used by all retrying utilities.

### Mass reads

- `readList(client, params)` — runs scan/query, returns `{items, nextParams}`.
  - `readList.getItems(client, params)` is the same; the export is a function with an attached helper.
- `readList.byKeys(client, table, keys, params)` — batchGet adapter that returns items in arbitrary order.
- `readListByKeys(client, table, keys, params)` — like above, exported separately.
- `readOrderedListByKeys(client, table, keys, params)` — preserves caller key order; used for indirect-index reads.
- `paginateList(client, params, options)` — offset/limit pagination, returns `{data, ...}`. `options` accepts `offset`, `limit`, `fields`.
- `paginateListNoLimit(client, params, options)` — variant without an upper bound.
- `iterateList(client, params)` — async iterator yielding pages.
- `getTotal(client, params)` — returns the Count via `Select=COUNT`.

### Mass writes

- `writeList(client, table, items, mapFn?)` — chunked `batchWrite` with backoff; `mapFn(item)` may transform each.
- `deleteList(client, params)` — scans and deletes everything matching params.
  - `deleteList.byKeys(client, table, keys)` — batched delete by key.
- `copyList(client, params, mapFn)` and `copyList.viaKeys(client, params, mapFn)` and `copyList.byKeys(client, table, keys, mapFn)` — read → mapFn → write.
- `moveList(client, params, mapFn)` / `moveList.viaKeys(...)` / `moveList.byKeys(...)` — read → mapFn → write → delete originals.

### Params helpers

- `addProjection(params, fields, projectionFieldMap?, skipSelect?)` — adds `ProjectionExpression`, dedupes attribute aliases, sets `Select=SPECIFIC_ATTRIBUTES` unless `skipSelect`.
- `cleanParams(params)` — drops empty `Expression*` members.
- `cloneParams(params)` — shallow clone using own-property check.
- `combineParams(a, b)` — merges two params, deduping attribute aliases.
- `filtering(filter, searchable, {fields, isDocClient, params})` — builds a `FilterExpression` for searchable substring matching.
- `normalizeFields(fields)` — `'a,b,c' | ['a','b']` → `['a','b','c']`.
- `subsetObject(obj, fields)` — returns a new object containing only listed fields.

### Patching helpers

- `prepareUpdate(patch, deleteProps, params, separator)` — see §3.
- `applyPatch(item, patch, deleteProps, separator)` — same semantics as `prepareUpdate` but operates on an in-memory object.
- `getPath(obj, path, separator?)` / `setPath(obj, path, value, separator?)` / `deletePath(obj, path, separator?)` — dotted-path object access.

### Misc

- `random` — small random helpers.
- `seq(promiseFns)` — runs an array of `() => Promise` sequentially.
- `sleep(ms)` — `await sleep(1000)`.

## 5. Test server

`tests/server.js` boots a Koa server on `localhost:3000` (env `HOST`, `PORT`). `tests/routes.js` wires an `Adapter` against table `test` keyed on `name`, plus a `KoaAdapter` overlay with `sortableIndices` and a `load` endpoint that re-imports `tests/data.json.gz` (Star Wars planets).

```bash
HOST=localhost PORT=3000 npm start
# then in another terminal:
curl -X PUT http://localhost:3000/-load
curl http://localhost:3000/Alderaan
```

The Postman collection at `tests/Unit test dynamodb-toolkit.postman_collection.json` exercises every endpoint. There is no `npm test`; run the collection in Postman or `newman` against the running server.

## 6. Conventions

- **CommonJS only.** `require` / `module.exports`. Files start with `'use strict';`.
- **Zero runtime dependencies.** The consumer supplies `aws-sdk`.
- **Prettier:** 160 char width, single quotes, no bracket spacing, no trailing commas, arrow parens "avoid".
- The wiki at https://github.com/uhop/dynamodb-toolkit/wiki is the user-facing reference. Page filenames use `:` for the section separator (e.g., `Adapter:-CRUD-methods.md`).
